ddls:
CREATE TABLE "universities" (
  "uni_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each university */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the university */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the university is located */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the university is located */,
  "university_type" TEXT /* Type of university (e.g., public, private, research) */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the university */,
  "founding_year" INTEGER /* Year the university was founded */,
  "teaching" REAL /* Teaching score */,
  "international" REAL /* International diversity score */,
  "research" REAL /* Research output score */,
  "citations" REAL /* Citations score */,
  "income" REAL /* Income */,
  "total_score" REAL /* Total score */,
  "num_faculty" INTEGER /* Number of faculty members */,
  "num_students" INTEGER /* Number of students */,
  "num_international_students" INTEGER /* Number of international students */,
  "num_academic_programs" INTEGER /* Number of academic programs offered */,
  "num_research_centers" INTEGER /* Number of research centers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("uni_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "uni_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university */,
  "num_students" INTEGER /* Number of students */,
  "student_staff_ratio" REAL /* Student-to-staff ratio */,
  "international_students" TEXT /* Percentage of international students */,
  "female_male_ratio" TEXT /* Female-to-male student ratio */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of data collection */,
  "num_faculty" INTEGER /* Number of faculty members */,
  "faculty_diversity" REAL /* Percentage of faculty from diverse backgrounds */,
  "research_publications" INTEGER /* Number of research publications */,
  "student_satisfaction" REAL /* Student satisfaction score */,
  "graduation_rate" REAL /* Graduation rate */,
  "employment_rate" REAL /* Employment rate of graduates */,
  "average_salary" REAL /* Average salary of graduates */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_uni_id FOREIGN KEY ("uni_id") REFERENCES universities ("uni_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rankings" (
  "rank_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ranking */,
  "uni_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university */,
  "world_rank" INTEGER /* World ranking */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of ranking */,
  "regional_rank" INTEGER /* Regional ranking */,
  "national_rank" INTEGER /* National ranking */,
  "ranking_methodology" TEXT /* Description of the ranking methodology used */,
  "rank_change" INTEGER /* Change in ranking from the previous year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rank_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rankings_uni_id FOREIGN KEY ("uni_id") REFERENCES universities ("uni_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password (hashed) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address */,
  "role" TEXT /* User role (e.g., administrator, data analyst, researcher) */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_university_id FOREIGN KEY ("university_id") REFERENCES universities ("uni_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collaborations" (
  "collaboration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaboration */,
  "uni_id_1" INTEGER /* ID of the first university */,
  "uni_id_2" INTEGER /* ID of the second university */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the collaboration project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the collaboration */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the collaboration */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the collaboration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collaboration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaborations_uni_id_1 FOREIGN KEY ("uni_id_1") REFERENCES universities ("uni_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaborations_uni_id_2 FOREIGN KEY ("uni_id_2") REFERENCES universities ("uni_id")
)
column_names: Country where the university is located
City where the university is located
Type of university (e.g., public, private, research)
Unique identifier for each performance metric
Percentage of faculty from diverse backgrounds
Description of the ranking methodology used
Change in ranking from the previous year
User role (e.g., administrator, data analyst, researcher)
